observations: Chrome control rejected the fresh file chooser before upload; no model request was sent for that attempt.
The single billable acceptance regenerated an existing user message that already referenced acceptance.xlsx.
CodeAPI logged the expected stale-reference GET 405 followed by POST /upload 200, but no /exec request.
The browser remained in generating state beyond the 45 second pre-parse bound.
Mongo showed no new assistant row for the stopped acceptance request, so the existing early-abort guard prevented another blank persisted sibling.

diagnosis.summary: The replacement fixed the stale-reference resolver in isolation but did not cover regeneration semantics. On regenerate, req.body.files is empty even though the original user message still owns the attachment; initializeAgent classifies that file as historical tool usage, currentRequestFiles remains empty, and the pre-parse gate bypasses before /exec. The resumable controller still leaves this initialization/provider path visibly generating instead of returning a bounded visible error.
required_follow_up: Resolve the regenerated parent user message and treat its attached file IDs as current-turn files for pre-parse selection.
Add controller-level coverage for original upload and regeneration request normalization.
Make every initializeClient/initializeAgent failure complete the GenerationJob with a visible error and no assistant placeholder.

Use a supported fresh-upload automation path or a server-side authenticated acceptance fixture; do not substitute regeneration for fresh-upload acceptance without explicitly testing its different file normalization contract.

decision: Do not redeploy this artifact. Implement regeneration-aware current-turn file resolution and controller-level initialization failure completion before another production write.
